title: Gerascanthus harrisii
intro: Gerascanthus harrisii, also known as Harris' Gerascanthus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ebenaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever reduction, and has been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Gerascanthus harrisii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Gerascanthus harrisii is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is traditionally used to make teas and tonics, as well as being used in some traditional remedies for fever and infection.
